parsed_review.summary: CP00-453 is a Risk-C, 150-unit descriptor-only closeout pack bootstrapping the RP15 "Firm Analytics" program (gates H15/C15, upstream RP14). The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly FALSE pending human final approval, and every reviewed artifact supports that posture. Implementation (packages/analytics/src/registry.js, service.js, validators.js, index.js) is purely descriptor/validator logic: grep confirms no runtime execution, network, fs writes, child_process, process.env, or eval; the frozen ANALYTICS_CORE_CP453_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION sets all dispatch/write/leak flags false. Tests are genuine: model.test.js (node:test + assert/strict) asserts coverage.valid===true, descriptor-only rows, and claude_is_final_approval===false; validate-rp15-analytics-core-contract.mjs deep-equals contract projections against the source-of-truth registry and pins historical bindings to CP00-453. command-evidence.json records 27 commands at exit 0 (node --check/--test, rp05-rp15 validators, plan/weighted/spec/control-plane/goal/fullplan validators, npm test/validate/build, git diff --check) with no_real_data:true and writes_product_state:false; Hermes gate H15 passed. Authority boundary is preserved across manifest, contract, and command-evidence: production_ready:false, status implemented_pending_review, claude_not_final_approver:true, enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation:true, and Hermes patterns confined to deterministic evidence-packet/receipt shape (no_hermes_runtime_receipt:true; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false). CP178+ review-hardening holds: no raw-output.json or review-receipt.json pre-exist (this is a genuine live run), pack_level_claude_review.status is pending with empty valid_review_receipts and invalid_review_attempts, the readiness auth-preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON, and the 2489-byte prompt carries no embedded diff/source and no forbidden tools. changed-file-scope.json cleanly separates the 10 active pack implementation refs from unrelated dirty files (docx, workbook/*, ldip invalid-attempt, handoff doc) with active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted empty. Deliverable distribution (implementation 102, contract 6, security_audit 10, ui 14, fixture 3, test 9, hermes_evidence 3, claude_review 3) and the micro-title distribution both reconcile to 150 against the baseline included_units. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and is not treated as final approval authority. No P0/P1/P2 issues; one P3 informational note about the production_ready_flag label and production_ready_candidate semantics, which are correctly subordinate to the false production_ready boolean and therefore non-blocking. Recommend the pack proceed to the post-review adjudication, construction inspection, and human-gated final closeout steps.

findings.title: production_ready_flag label and production_ready_candidate:true could be misread as a readiness claim
findings.details: The pack binding carries production_ready_flag: "analytics_core_scope_contract_foundation_descriptor_verified" (registry.js:13) and createAnalyticsCoreCp453HermesEvidencePacket computes production_ready_candidate: coverage.valid && foundation.valid, which evaluates true (service.js:190). In isolation these positively-worded fields could be misread as asserting production/enterprise readiness. In context they are correctly subordinate: the boolean production_ready is false in manifest.json (line 6) and command-evidence.json (line 6), runtime_ready is false, status is implemented_pending_review, and enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation/claude_not_final_approver are true. The label is a descriptor-verification marker and the candidate flag feeds the human-gated closeout, not a final production claim. No actual over-claim of production readiness or enterprise trust is present.
findings.recommendation: Optional: keep the explicit boolean production_ready:false as the authoritative gate and consider renaming/annotating the *_flag and *_candidate descriptor labels (e.g. descriptor_verification_label) in a future pack to remove any ambiguity. No change required for CP00-453 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Accepted as non-blocking informational. Verified that the authoritative production_ready boolean is false across manifest, command-evidence, and contract, and that enterprise trust and Claude final-approval are explicitly disclaimed; the label/candidate fields do not constitute a production or enterprise-trust over-claim.
